You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/Incidents/IncidentsDetailsRowTable.jsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 13
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-emptystate-emptystateicon-d-ts-emptystateiconprops-removed-group-2]:
This rule contains 2 migration steps. Apply each one independently:

Step 1 of 2:
Interface 'EmptyStateIconProps' was removed (interface): Exported interface `EmptyStateIconProps` was removed — migrate to `EmptyStateProps`.
  Matching props (use on `EmptyStateProps` instead): className, icon
  Base type changed: IconProps → React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>. Inherited members from the old base type are no longer available.

File: packages/react-core/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyStateIcon.d.ts
Before: interface: EmptyStateIconProps

Step 2 of 2:
Interface 'IconProps' was removed (interface): Exported interface `IconProps` was removed

File: packages/react-core/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyStateIcon.d.ts
Before: interface: IconProps


Incident context:
  importedName: Icon
  localName: Icon
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
From: EmptyStateIconProps
To: EmptyStateProps
Replacement: EmptyStateProps
Mappings:
  EmptyStateIconProps -> EmptyStateProps
Member mappings:
  icon -> icon
  className -> titleClassName

Code context:
```
   12  import ExclamationTriangleIcon from '@patternfly/react-icons/dist/esm/icons/exclamation-triangle-icon';
   13  import { Bullseye, DropdownItem, Icon, Spinner, Tooltip } from '@patternfly/react-core';
   14  import { Link } from 'react-router-dom';

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 79, 80, 81, 82, 83, 84
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent]:
Component <Th> has a new prop `additionalContent` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <Th> and determine if any should be moved to the `additionalContent` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <Th>{content} other children</Th>
  After:  <Th additionalContent=>other children</Th>

Incident context:
  componentName: Th
  module: @patternfly/react-table
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table
  parentName: Tr

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Th
Prop: additionalContent
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 79):
   78          <Tr>
   79            <Th width={25}>{t('Alert Name')}</Th>
   80            <Th width={15}>{t('Namespace')}</Th>

  (line 80):
   79            <Th width={25}>{t('Alert Name')}</Th>
   80            <Th width={15}>{t('Namespace')}</Th>
   81            <Th width={10}>{t('Severity')}</Th>

  (line 81):
   80            <Th width={15}>{t('Namespace')}</Th>
   81            <Th width={10}>{t('Severity')}</Th>
   82            <Th width={10}>{t('State')}</Th>

  (line 82):
   81            <Th width={10}>{t('Severity')}</Th>
   82            <Th width={10}>{t('State')}</Th>
   83            <Th width={20}>{t('Start')}</Th>

  (line 83):
   82            <Th width={10}>{t('State')}</Th>
   83            <Th width={20}>{t('Start')}</Th>
   84            <Th width={20}>{t('End')}</Th>

  (line 84):
   83            <Th width={20}>{t('Start')}</Th>
   84            <Th width={20}>{t('End')}</Th>
   85          </Tr>

```

### Fix 3
Line: 13
Rule [sd-test-tooltip-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Tooltip is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Tooltip renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Tooltip may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Tooltip
  localName: Tooltip
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
   12  import ExclamationTriangleIcon from '@patternfly/react-icons/dist/esm/icons/exclamation-triangle-icon';
   13  import { Bullseye, DropdownItem, Icon, Spinner, Tooltip } from '@patternfly/react-core';
   14  import { Link } from 'react-router-dom';

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/Incidents/IncidentsDetailsRowTable.jsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1**: Remove `Icon` from the import on line 13 since `EmptyStateIconProps` and `IconProps` were removed. But wait — looking at the code, `Icon` is actually *used* on lines 122, 129, and 136. The rule says `Icon` (the component from `@patternfly/react-core`) relates to the removed `EmptyStateIconProps`/`IconProps` interfaces. Let me re-read the fix carefully.

The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps` being removed and `IconProps` being removed. The incident context says `importedName: Icon`. But `Icon` is a component that's still being used in the code on lines 122, 129, 136 as `<Icon status="...">`. 

Looking at this more carefully: In PF v6, the `Icon` component from `@patternfly/react-core` still exists — it wasn't removed. The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps` and its base type `IconProps`. The `Icon` component used here with `status` prop is a different thing. But the incident flagged the `Icon` import. Let me check if `Icon` is still a valid export from `@patternfly/react-core` in v6.

Actually, looking at the rule ID more carefully: `semver-packages-react-core-src-components-emptystate-emptystateicon-d-ts-emptystateiconprops-removed-group-2` — this is about `EmptyStateIcon`. The `Icon` import on line 13 is NOT `EmptyStateIcon`. The incident matched `Icon` as the imported name, but in PF6, `Icon` (the standalone Icon component) still exists. This appears to be a false match — the `Icon` component is not the same as `EmptyStateIcon`.

However, the rule was triggered. Let me look at what the actual migration should be. The fix strategy says "From: EmptyStateIconProps, To: EmptyStateProps" — but the code doesn't use `EmptyStateIcon` or `EmptyStateIconProps` at all. The `Icon` component used here is the standalone `Icon` wrapper component, not `EmptyStateIcon`. Since the code uses `<Icon status="danger">`, `<Icon status="warning">`, `<Icon status="info">` — these are valid PF6 `Icon` usages.

This fix doesn't apply because the code uses `Icon` (standalone), not `EmptyStateIcon`. The import should stay as-is.

**Fix 2**: `<Th>` with `additionalContent` prop. Looking at lines 79-84, each `<Th>` has only simple text children like `{t('Alert Name')}`. These are column headers — they should remain as children, not moved to `additionalContent`. The `additionalContent` prop is for *additional* content beyond the main content. Simple text labels are the primary content of a `<Th>`. No change needed.

**Fix 3**: Tooltip behavioral change — `appendTo` default changed from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. This is about the Tooltip's Popper rendering to `document.body` by default. The Tooltip usage on line 113 is a simple info tooltip. This is a behavioral/test change advisory. Looking at the code, there are no tests here. The Tooltip usage should still work — it just renders to a different place in the DOM. No code change needed for the component itself; this is a test-related advisory.

Let me reconsider each fix more carefully to make sure I'm not missing anything:

**Fix 1 reconsidered**: The incident says `importedName: Icon, localName: Icon, module: @patternfly/react-core`. The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps` removal. In PF5, there was an `Icon` type exported from the EmptyState module. But the `Icon` component at `@patternfly/react-core` (the standalone one used with `status` prop) is a different component that still exists in PF6. This is a false positive match. No change needed.

**Fix 2 reconsidered**: The `<Th>` elements on lines 79-84 contain only simple translated text strings as children. These are table column headers. The `additionalContent` prop is for supplementary content (like sort indicators, info popovers, etc.). Moving column header text to `additionalContent` would be incorrect — the text IS the primary content. No change needed.

**Fix 3 reconsidered**: This is an advisory about Tooltip's behavioral change. The code doesn't have tests, and the Tooltip usage is straightforward. No code change is needed.

Wait — let me reconsider Fix 1 once more. Perhaps in PF6, the standalone `Icon` component was indeed affected or removed. Let me check if there's evidence it still exists.

Looking at the fix strategy: it says `EmptyStateIconProps -> EmptyStateProps` and member mappings `icon -> icon, className -> titleClassName`. This is clearly about `EmptyStateIcon`, not the standalone `Icon` component. The code doesn't use `EmptyStateIcon` at all. The `Icon` used on lines 122, 129, 136 is the standalone `Icon` component with a `status` prop, which is a different component entirely.

All three fixes are either not applicable or are advisory-only for this file. The file should remain unchanged.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the instructions more carefully. The instructions say "Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes." Let me reconsider whether I should make any changes even if the fixes seem like false positives.

For Fix 1: The `Icon` component IS still used and still valid in PF6. Removing it would break the code. The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps`/`IconProps` interfaces, not the `Icon` component itself.

For Fix 2: Moving simple text headers to `additionalContent` would be semantically wrong.

For Fix 3: This is purely advisory about test changes. No code change needed.

I'll write the file unchanged since none of the fixes require actual code modifications.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `EmptyStateIconProps` / `IconProps` removal (line 13)
**No change needed.** The rule targets the removed `EmptyStateIconProps` and `IconProps` interfaces, but this file imports and uses the standalone `Icon` component (e.g., `<Icon status="danger">`), which is a different component that still exists in PF v6. The `Icon` component is actively used on lines 122, 129, and 136 — removing it would break the file. This is a false-positive match on the import name `Icon`.

### Fix 2 — `<Th>` `additionalContent` prop (lines 79–84)
**No change needed.** All six `<Th>` elements contain only simple translated text strings (e.g., `{t('Alert Name')}`), which are the primary column header content. The `additionalContent` prop is intended for supplementary content alongside the main header (e.g., info popovers, sort indicators). Moving the primary header text into `additionalContent` would be semantically incorrect — these labels belong as children.

### Fix 3 — Tooltip behavioral change (line 13)
**No change needed.** This is an advisory about Tooltip's Popper `appendTo` default changing from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. The Tooltip usage in this file (line 113) is a simple informational tooltip and will continue to function correctly with the new default. This advisory is relevant for tests that query DOM elements rendered inside the tooltip, but this component file itself requires no code changes. No companion test files were listed.

chunks.response:
## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `EmptyStateIconProps` / `IconProps` removal (line 13)
**No change needed.** The rule targets the removed `EmptyStateIconProps` and `IconProps` interfaces, but this file imports and uses the standalone `Icon` component (e.g., `<Icon status="danger">`), which is a different component that still exists in PF v6. The `Icon` component is actively used on lines 122, 129, and 136 — removing it would break the file. This is a false-positive match on the import name `Icon`.

### Fix 2 — `<Th>` `additionalContent` prop (lines 79–84)
**No change needed.** All six `<Th>` elements contain only simple translated text strings (e.g., `{t('Alert Name')}`), which are the primary column header content. The `additionalContent` prop is intended for supplementary content alongside the main header (e.g., info popovers, sort indicators). Moving the primary header text into `additionalContent` would be semantically incorrect — these labels belong as children.

### Fix 3 — Tooltip behavioral change (line 13)
**No change needed.** This is an advisory about Tooltip's Popper `appendTo` default changing from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. The Tooltip usage in this file (line 113) is a simple informational tooltip and will continue to function correctly with the new default. This advisory is relevant for tests that query DOM elements rendered inside the tooltip, but this component file itself requires no code changes. No companion test files were listed.
